name: The Liberty Arena
about: The Liberty Arena is a sports and entertainment complex in Central Pennsylvania, featuring a golf simulator alongside various other activities like indoor soccer, batting cages, and a family fun zone. The facility offers a full restaurant and bar through Scorz Bar & Grill, making it a destination for both sports and social events. Golfers can access the simulator for practice and play, with opportunities for leagues, coaching, and group rentals.
why_golfers_like_it: Indoor golf simulator available for year-round practice
On-site restaurant and bar for a complete experience
Available for group events and corporate outings
Part of a larger complex with multiple sports and entertainment options

food_and_drink.notes: American cuisine available at Scorz Bar & Grill
Full bar with rotating craft beers

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify, but it is recommended to bring your own clubs for the golf simulator experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has an on-site restaurant and bar, Scorz Bar & Grill, which serves American cuisine and has a full bar. Outside food and drink policies are not mentioned.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide information on the time it takes to play 18 holes on the simulator.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, The Liberty Arena is kid-friendly, offering various attractions in the Kaos Fun Zone, including a trampoline park, laser tag, and arcade games.
